answer:
The angular position of the first maximum is given by \(d \sin\theta = m\lambda\), so \(\sin\theta \approx \theta = \frac{\lambda}{d} = \frac{600 \times 10^{-9}}{0.10 \times 10^{-3}} = 0.006\) radians. The distance on the screen is \(y = L\theta = 2.00 \times 0.006 = 0.012\) m = 12 mm.  

\boxed{F}
